data.title: Moderna Stock Drops After Earnings Report. What Has Wall Street Worried. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Josh Nathan-Kazis

Moderna is due to report its second-quarter financial results on Friday ahead of the market open, a day after announcing plans to cut 10% of its staff as the company's cash hoard continues to shrink.

Moderna's second-quarter performance is largely beside the point. Everyone knows that sales of the company's Covid-19 vaccine have tanked. Instead, investors are focused on the big question looming over the stock: Can Moderna stop losing money before it runs out of cash?

Shares of the biotech have dropped 26.3% this year to $30.58. At its peak in August of 2021, the stock was trading at more than $480. The company had nearly $20 billion in cash as of early 2022, but it's been losing billions of dollars per year since 2023, when the market for its Covid-19 vaccine began to shrink dramatically.

Moderna said in May that it will have $6 billion in cash left at the end of this year, though analysts are doubtful: The FactSet consensus estimate is currently $3.9 billion.

The company's CEO, Stéphane Bancel, has insisted that the company will stop losing money in 2028, and won't need to raise more cash before then.

Investors will be watching Moderna's financial report Friday for signs as to whether that's achievable.

The staff reductions announced Thursday show the company is working on cost cuts. Moderna had said in May that it was cutting annual operating expenses by $1.5 billion by 2027. The staff reductions will bring its headcount below 5,000 employees by the end of the year.

"We see the ongoing restructuring effort as necessary to right-size the company's operational footprint to offset continued vaccine revenue decline," Leerink partners analyst Mani Foroohar wrote on Thursday.

Analysts expect Moderna to report sales of $113 million for the second quarter, according to FactSet, down from $241 million in 2024 and $4.7 billion in 2022. They expect a net loss on the quarter of $1.1 billion.

The FactSet consensus estimate has Moderna losing $2.96 per share in the second quarter. The company lost $3.33 per share in the same quarter last year.

Write to Josh Nathan-Kazis at josh.nathan-kazis@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Oil Prices Fall on Renewed Trade Tensions, Oversupply Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Oil prices fall, with Brent crude and WTI both down 1% at $71.01 a barrel and $68.55 a barrel respectively. Oil prices are higher on week but pressure could reassert itself as rising inventories and renewed trade tensions weigh on market sentiment, XTB MENA analysts say in a note. A surprise 7.7 million U.S. crude stock build has raised fresh concerns of slowing demand while cartel OPEC+ is set to increase production in September, which could weigh on prices, analysts say. President Trump's decision to impose import tariffs ranging from 10% to 41% on key trading partners has also reignited concerns of slower global growth and weaker fuel demand. The threat of secondary tariffs on Russian oil have raised the specter of disrupted trade flows, but broader economic protectionism implications could outweigh any positive effect, analysts add. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: Nonfarm Payrolls: What Counts as a Jobs Numbers Whiff? — WSJ
data.description:
By Spencer Jakab

This is an online version of Spencer's Markets A.M. newsletter. Get investing insights in your inbox each weekday by signing up here-it's free.

Baseball broadcasts have changed, and it isn't just all the gambling ads: Coverage now comes with an explosion of acronyms and statistics that never graced the back of a bubble-gum card.

Like an old-school fan, though, Fed Chair Jerome Powell said on Wednesday that "the main number you have to look at" in assessing the economy now is the unemployment rate-an everyman figure that's like the batting average of jobs data. That isn't due to lack of sophistication. Unemployment, which is expected to have risen a tenth of a point in July to 4.2%, is telling because other numbers are starting to mean less.

Economists put a lot of emphasis on the change in nonfarm payrolls, which is an estimate drawn from the separate "establishment" survey. Those polled by The Wall Street Journal expect it to rise by 100,000 jobs in this morning's report-the least since September. The measure averaged 130,000 so far this year, 168,000 last year and 216,000 the year before that.

Mild payroll deceleration probably won't send the unemployment rate much higher, though. So the Fed-at least while Powell is chair-might not be spooked into slashing rates. An average of 100,000 jobs a month would keep unemployment at a respectable 4.2% a year from now, according to a calculator from the Atlanta Fed.

The odds of a rate cut in September fell following Powell's press conference on Wednesday when the Fed held rates steady, according to CME FedWatch. Don't misread him, though-it also depends on why unemployment is holding steady. Will the economy be like a batter making contact but not hitting for power?

Payrolls could grow even more slowly without spiking unemployment if the labor-force participation rate falls too-a possible side effect of much less immigration. But if private-sector employers also slow hiring because the economy just isn't strong enough to require more workers, that's a different matter.

Powell explained his thinking on the unemployment rate: "The fact that it's getting into balance due to declines in both supply and demand, though...is suggestive of downside risk. So...of course we'll be watching that carefully."

One of the Fed's official goals is "maximum employment." It still won't sit idly by if other information tells it the economy is slumping. Data points like the monthly Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey and consumer polls about the ease of finding jobs aren't as rosy as they were earlier this year.

Watch unemployment, but don't take your eye off the ball.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Latest on U.S. Politics
data.description:
Market Talks covering the impact of U.S. Politics and White House policies on companies and markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0724 ET - Oil prices fall, with Brent crude and WTI both down 1% at $71.01 a barrel and $68.55 a barrel respectively. Oil prices are higher on week but pressure could reassert itself as rising inventories and renewed trade tensions weigh on market sentiment, XTB MENA analysts say in a note. A surprise 7.7 million U.S. crude stock build has raised fresh concerns of slowing demand while cartel OPEC+ is set to increase production in September, which could weigh on prices, analysts say. President Trump's decision to impose import tariffs ranging from 10% to 41% on key trading partners has also reignited concerns of slower global growth and weaker fuel demand. The threat of secondary tariffs on Russian oil have raised the specter of disrupted trade flows, but broader economic protectionism implications could outweigh any positive effect, analysts add. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0710 ET - Switzerland faces a significant hit to its economy from U.S. trade tariffs, Capital Economics' Adrian Prettejohn warns. The Swiss government said Friday it is "analyzing the situation" after President Trump said the U.S. would slap 39% duties on Swiss imports, which range from pharmaceuticals to luxury watches and machinery, though pharma products appear to be exempted for now from the higher tariffs. That threatens to reduce the export-oriented Swiss economy by some 0.6%, according to Capital Economics' calculations. And if pharma is eventually hit by 39% duties, the drag would be significantly more, Prettejohn says. The Swiss economy will continue to grow under those circumstances, but by a notably slower rate, he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0641 ET - Long-dated U.S. Treasury yields rise ahead of U.S. nonfarm payrolls data which are likely to confirm the resilience of the U.S. economy, says RBC BlueBay Asset Management's Mark Dowding in a note. "We would expect this narrative [of a robust U.S. economy] to continue to be reaffirmed in the monthly jobs report," says Dowding, who is BlueBay's CIO. Near-term inflation risks due to U.S. tariffs mean there is therefore limited reason to expect Treasury yields to decline any time soon, he says. President Trump on Thursday said the U.S. would raise tariffs on multiple trading partners on Aug. 7. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 3.4 basis points to 4.394%, while the 30-year yield is up 4.6 basis points at 4.931%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0626 ET - The cost of insuring euro-denominated junk bonds against default using credit default swaps climbs as sentiment deteriorates on tariff concerns. President Trump on Thursday said the U.S. would raise tariffs on multiple trading partners on Aug. 7.. "While agreements covering roughly 60% of U.S. imports have been secured since the deadline was extended, significant gaps remain that threaten major economic disruption," Mattioli Woods' Katie Stoves says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps rises 4 basis points to last trade at 273bps, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0521 ET - The Canadian dollar falls to a 10-week low against its U.S. counterpart after President Trump announced that Canada will face tariffs of 35% on any exports into the U.S., having failed to agree a trade deal. Canada's currency could fall even further, particularly given that Mexico secured another pause extension, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. "We think markets continue to underestimate the downside risks for the Canadian economy," the FX strategist says. The tariff announcement increases the risk of more interest-rate cuts by the Bank of Canada, he says. The U.S. dollar rises 0.2% to a high of 1.3876 Canadian dollars, and ING expects it could reach 1.40 Canadian dollars in the near term.(jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0511 ET - Base metal prices fall, with LME three-month copper down 0.2% at $9,590.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.4% at $2,552.50 a ton. Metal prices slumped across the board this week as markets digested U.S. trade announcements, a stronger dollar and sluggish mainland China demand, BMI analysts say in a note. Copper and aluminum are down 2.6% and 3.5% on week respectively. Copper traded in New York slumped more than 20% on Thursday after President Trump's administration said tariffs wouldn't apply to refined copper imports, wiping out weeks of gains and significantly reducing the U.S. premium over LME prices, BMI analysts say. Other base metals, including aluminum and nickel slid as Chinese demand continues to disappoint, BMI adds. LME three-month nickel is down 3.9% on week at $14,865 a metric ton. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0506 ET - Apple might have to navigate a host of challenges in the coming months even though its latest quarterly results show the company is faring better than feared, UBS analysts write in a note to clients. The iPhone maker is grappling with competition from local brands in China, analysts say. Apple also faces potential tariffs as an investigation by the Trump administration could result in specific levies for semiconductors, they add. The risk that the Department of Justice changes Apple's revenue agreement with Alphabet's Google when it is expected to rule in August also remains, analysts say. Apple shares are up 1.5% premarket at $210.73 after strong quarterly results. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0501 ET - The Swiss franc falls to a 5-week low against the U.S. dollar after President Trump announced 39% tariffs on Swiss exports to the U.S., due to come into effect from August 7. This is higher than the 31% which Trump had threatened in April. The franc also weakens against the euro as concerns about the economic impact of tariffs outweigh the Swiss currency's safe-haven status. The U.S. dollar rises 0.5% to a high of 0.8169 francs, LSEG data show. The euro rises 0.5% to 0.9318 francs. Concerns about tariffs are now overtaking the previous tech-driven optimism on markets, Tickmill Group's Patrick Munnelly says in a note. Switzerland's SMI stock-market index falls 0.8%. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0422 ET - The reaction on markets to U.S. tariffs on trade partners such as Canada and Switzerland has been relatively muted due to the fact that trade agreements have been sealed with Japan and the European Union, IG analysts say in a note. "Markets were relatively calm as key deals with Japan and the EU were already secured, and talks with China and Mexico continue," they say. The pan European Stoxx 600 index declines 1.1% to 539.84 points. Switzerland's SMI index falls 0.8%. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0402 ET - Oil prices are up significantly on week, with Brent and WTI 6% and 6.3% higher, respectively. With a better growth backdrop from positive economic data releases and concerns over tightening Russian supply in July, Brent crude posted its best month since September 2023, Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. Oil prices rallied late in the month after President Trump threatened new sanctions or secondary tariffs against importers of Russian oil, with a deadline of Aug. 8, analysts write. U.S. trade policy also appeared more successful over July, with a raft of trade deals signed late in the month, raising economic hopes and optimism for oil demand. In early morning trade, Brent crude is flat at $71.69 a barrel and WTI is down 0.1% at $69.22 a barrel. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0341 ET - European stocks fall sharply in early trade, led by losses in the healthcare and insurance sectors, after President Trump signed an executive order Thursday night significantly raising tariffs on scores of nations. Trump also sent letters to 17 big pharmaceutical companies asking them to lower costs of drugs for U.S. consumers. The Stoxx Europe 600 falls 1%, with the insurance and healthcare sector indexes down 1.6% and 1.5%, respectively. Germany's DAX, France's CAC 40 and Italy's FTSE MIB all drop more than 1%. Markets are closed in Switzerland--which will be hit with a 39% tariff, Trump's order said--due to a local holiday. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0328 ET - Shares of European pharmaceutical companies fell in early trade after U.S. President Trump sent out letters Thursday asking them to cut the costs of drugs for U.S. customers to the lowest offered in other developed nations.Shares of GSK, AstraZeneca, Novo Nordisk, and Sanofi were down 1.7%, 2.8%, 4.2% and 1.2%, respectively. The Stoxx Europe 600 healthcare index was down 1.8%. The President said he would use "every tool in our arsenal" to protect American families from high prices if companies didn't act voluntarily. (ian.walker@wsj.com.)

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive Aims to Bolster Productivity as 2Q Profit, Revenue Rise
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Colgate-Palmolive said it will undergo efforts to streamline and reduce costs as it continues to operate in what Chief Executive Noel Wallace called a volatile and uncertain market, even as it posted higher second-quarter profit and revenue.

The maker of Ajax and Softsoap on Friday posted a profit of $743 million, or 91 cents a share, compared with $731 million, or 89 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings were 92 cents a share, compared with the 90 cents a share that analysts polled by FactSet expected.

Revenue ticked 1% higher to $5.11 billion, topping the $5.04 billion that Wall Street projected.

The New York consumer-products maker backed its full-year outlook for net sales to be up in the low-single digits. Organic sales growth is now expected to come in at the low end of its 2%-to-4% range. The company noted that its outlook numbers include current tariff rates, a negative impact from foreign exchange and its planned exit from private-label pet sales.

Wallace said the company continues to face difficult market conditions worldwide.

Separately, Colgate-Palmolive disclosed a three-year productivity program, which the company said will better align its organizational structure, optimize its global supply chain and streamline its organizational structure to reduce overhead costs. The company expects to record $200 million to $300 million in charges over the course of the program.

Shares climbed 0.9% to $84.61 in premarket trading.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: McDonald's plans to 'double down' on AI investment by 2027, executive says
data.description:
McDonald's plans to "double down" on its artificial intelligence investments by 2027 and is betting on India to be a key hub for data governance, engineering and platform architecture, a senior executive said on Friday.

The fast-food giant, which entered India in 1996, operates hundreds of restaurants across the country and recently set up a global office in the southern city of Hyderabad, with an aim to make it the largest outside the United States.

"We're still in the early stages, so it's hard to pin down the exact investment," McDonald's head of Global Business Services operations, Deshant Kaila, said in an interview on the sidelines of an event in Hyderabad.

McDonald's is using AI to verify orders at 400 restaurants to pre-empt errors before handing them over to customers, and expects to roll this out to 40,000 locations globally by 2027, Durga Prakash, head of technology (global offices), said.

The fast-food giant is also using AI tools to forecast sales, decide on pricing and assess product performance and is building a personalised app, which would work across countries, according to Kaila.

He said the India push will centre on building its AI team, but added that spending will lean more toward technology and tools, not headcount.

The company is in talks to set up a global office in Poland, just like the ones in India and Mexico, according to Durga Prakash.

Earlier this year, the southern Indian state of Telangana said that McDonald's would launch a global capability center, employing 2,000 people in Hyderabad.

India's global capability centers, once low-cost outsourcing hubs for global corporations, have evolved to support their parent organisations in domains ranging from operations and finance to research and development.

data.title: Nexera Announces Closing of Private Placement of Units
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta, and San Antonio, Texas--(Newsfile Corp. - August 1, 2025) - Nexera Energy Inc. (OTC Pink: EMBYF) (the "Corporation", the "Company" or "Nexera") today reported that the Corporation has closed its previously announced non-brokered private placement. Pursuant to this closing, an aggregate 40,000,002 units ("Units") were issued (of which 5,500,000 Units were acquired by a directors/officers of the Corporation) at a price of $0.015 per Unit, for aggregate consideration of $600,000. Each Unit consisted of one (1) Common Share of the Corporation and one (1) share purchase warrant (the "Warrant") (each full Warrant shall entitle the holder thereof to purchase one (1) additional Common Share of the Corporation for a period of 24 months from the issuance of the Units at a price of $0.10) (the "Offering").

The Warrants are subject to an acceleration clause whereby if after four months and one day following the date the Warrants are issued, the closing price of the Common Shares of the Corporation on the principal market on which such shares trade is equal to or exceeds $0.15 for 30 consecutive trading days (with the 30th such trading date hereafter referred to as the "Eligible Acceleration Date"), the Warrant expiry date shall accelerate to the date which is 30 calendar days following the date a press release is issued by the Corporation announcing the reduced warrant term, provided, no more than five business days following the Eligible Acceleration Date: (i) the press release is issued; and (ii) notices are sent to all warrant holders.

The net proceeds of the Offering will be utilized within the Corporation's wholly owned subsidiary, Production Resources Inc. ("PRI"), to grow the Company's oil and gas services opportunity in addition to a well optimization program at the Corporation's Taylor Ina and Somerset fields to bring approximately thirty inactive wells online. PRI will use net proceeds from the Offering to acquire additional equipment to expand the cement services branch within PRI to include additional well servicing operations. The additional equipment acquired will benefit and service both the Corporation on its own wells (owned through PRI), while at the same time allowing for additional revenues to be potentially generated by assisting other operators in the area with the servicing of their wells (of which management of the Corporation believes there to be a high demand for in the area).

Specifically, the Corporation anticipates using the net proceeds as follows: (i) equipment purchases and refurbishing - $140,000; (ii) well optimization program - $280,000; (iii) labour - $100,000; and (vi) any balance leftover would go to working capital purposes.

All of the Common Shares and Warrants issued pursuant to the private placement are subject to a four-month hold period. The Warrants will not be listed on any stock exchange. Completion of this Offering remains subject to the final approval of the TSX Venture Exchange.

For further information, please contact:

Nexera Energy Inc. President, Shelby D. Beattie, by telephone at (403) 262-6000

Email: info@nexeraenergy.com

www.nexeraenergy.com

About Nexera Energy Inc.

Nexera Energy Inc. is an energy company with oil producing properties in Southwest Texas. Nexera is owner and operator of the Lavernia, Wooden Horse and Stockdale Horizon Projects.. The Company also now owns 100% of Production Resources Inc., a South Texas oil company.

Forward-Looking Statements

data.title: TSX futures fall after Trump slaps fresh tariffs on Canada
data.description:
Futures tied to Canada's main stock index slipped on Friday, as investors woke up to U.S. President Donald Trump's new tariff regime that included fresh levies on Canada and dozens of other countries.

Futures on the S&P/TSX index (SXFcv1) were down 1% at 1,605.40 points by 06:52 a.m. ET (1052 GMT). The benchmark index gave back some of its monthly gains in the previous session.

Trump late on Thursday signed an executive order increasing tariffs on all Canadian goods not covered by the U.S.-Mexico-Canada trade agreement to 35% from 25%.

He also imposed steep tariffs on imports from dozens of other trading partners including Brazil, India, Taiwan and Switzerland, pressing ahead with his plans to reorder the global economy.

Adding to investors' concerns, Trump sent letters to the leaders of 17 major pharmaceutical companies outlining how they should slash U.S. prescription drug prices to match those paid overseas.

In commodities, gold prices held steady on Friday, while oil prices were little changed and copper prices stabilised.

In corporate news, Canadian auto parts supplier Magna International raised its annual sales forecast and topped second-quarter estimates, benefiting from its cost-cutting measures.

Focus will be on the U.S. jobs data, due later in the day.

FOR CANADIAN MARKETS NEWS, CLICK ON CODES:

TSX market report

Canadian dollar and bonds report

Reuters global stocks poll for Canada (EQUITYPOLL1), (EPOLL/CA)

Canadian markets directory (CANADA)

data.title: Moderna falls after lowering 2025 sales forecast
data.description:
** Shares of vaccine maker Moderna fall 4.4% to $28.25 premarket

** Co cuts 2025 sales forecast to $1.5 billion to $2.2 billion, cutting $300 million from the top end as UK revenue deliveries shifted into early 2026

** Reports quarterly revenue of $142 million, down 41% from last year, but ahead of analysts estimates of $112.9 million, as per data compiled by LSEG

** Co on Thursday said it would cut around 10% of its global workforce, shrinking to under 5,000 employees by year’s end

** Reports Q2 loss of $2.13 per share, less than analysts estimates of a loss of $2.97 per share

** Up to last close, MRNA down 28.9% YTD

data.title: Aditya Birla Housing accepts bids for 10-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's Aditya Birla Housing Finance accepts bids worth 15 billion rupees ($171.47 million) for bonds maturing in 10 years, three bankers said on Friday.

The company will pay an annual coupon of 7.58% and had invited bids from bankers and investors for the issue earlier in the day, they said.

The company did not reply to a Reuters email for comment.

data.title: Lost Money on RxSight, Inc. (RXST)? Join Class Action Before September 22, 2025 - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 1, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your RxSight, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/rxsight-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=159198&wire=1&utm_campaign=23

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against RxSight, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 7, 2024 and July 8, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) the Company was experiencing "adoption challenges" and/or structural issues resulting in declines in sales and utilization; (2) defendants had overstated the demand for RxSight's products; (3) as a result, RxSight was unlikely to meet its own previously issued financial guidance for fiscal year 2025; and (4) that, as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in RxSight, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/rxsight-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=159198&wire=1&utm_campaign=23 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

Fax: (212) 363-7171

https://zlk.com/

data.title: Aquestive Therapeutics Inc expected to post a loss of 17 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Aquestive Therapeutics Inc is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 4 (estimated) for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Warren New Jersey-based company is expected to report a 43.7% decrease in revenue to $11.32 million from $20.1 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 9 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Aquestive Therapeutics Inc is for a loss of 17 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 9 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Aquestive Therapeutics Inc is $8.00, about 52% above its last closing price of $3.84

data.title: Albany International Cut to Hold From Buy by Truist Securities
Assertio Holdings Inc expected to post a loss of 8 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Assertio Holdings Inc is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 (estimated) for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Lake Forest Illinois-based company is expected to report a 10.5% decrease in revenue to $27.858 million from $31.13 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Assertio Holdings Inc is for a loss of 8 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Assertio Holdings Inc is $3.00, about 76.1% above its last closing price of $0.72

Net Asset Value*

As at close of business on 31st July 2025 the unaudited net asset value per share, calculated in accordance with the AIC formula (including current year deficit and based on bid market values) was 442.66p.

* It should be noted that the NAV does not reflect any changes to markets in jurisdictions that may be closed on a given day, whether for public holidays or otherwise, but will reflect the last available market value from such jurisdiction.
